qas.q: Chemicals are classified as health hazards when they pose which of the following hazardous effects?
The Hazard Communication Standard was modified to adopt the Globally Harmonized System to:
qas.a: Provide a standardized approach to hazard communication

qas.q: All container of hazardous chemicals leaving the workplace must be labeled, tagged, or marked with the following information except?
qas.a: Name, address, and telephone number of employees using chemical at worksite

qas.q: The ___________ pictogram is used to warn about the oxidizer class of chemical hazards.
Hazard Communication covers both __________ and health hazard.
Employers should follow these three steps for putting a Hazard Communication program into practice:
qas.a: Identify responsible staff, identify hazardous chemicals, and prepare and implement the program

qas.q: Sections _____________ of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) contains general information about the chemical, identification, hazards, composition, safe handling practices, and emergency control measures.

If a chemical manufacturer, importer, or employer becomes aware of any significant information regarding the hazards of a chemical or ways to protect against hazards, this new information must be added to the SDS within__________ months.
Which of the following is the general definition of Hazard Communication?
qas.a: Hazard Communication is all about analyzing chemical components and testing for chemical hazard to ensure a safe and healthy work environment.

qas.q: Chemicals in the _________ class are liquids which, even in small quantities, are liable to ignite within five minutes after coming into contact with air.
